response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web developer with broad language exposure and some embedded/low-level experience, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of any Japanese language proficiency — a hard, explicitly stated requirement. Beyond that, the candidate has no demonstrable experience with network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), no LAN/switch or Layer-2/3 networking background, and C programming appears only peripherally across a career dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and web-focused work. Linux kernel investigation and modification capability, another core requirement, is not evidenced anywhere in the resume.
strengths: Broad systems exposure including embedded work (robotic arms, DLang low-latency services, NI LabWindows C/C++) shows some capacity outside pure web development
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) indicate disciplined engineering practices
Demonstrated Linux and Docker operational familiarity, along with experience maintaining dev infrastructure
concerns: Japanese language proficiency is entirely absent — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement for the role
No security domain experience whatsoever: zero coverage of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication, or any network security protocol
C programming is peripheral and dated (NI LabWindows entry); primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/web, incompatible with embedded LAN-switch firmware development
